text: The leading vehicle drives until it detects an obstacle, then applies full brakes.

Ego vehicle's straight-line traversal and the pedestrian's triggered crossing behavior at the intersection exit. The adversarial pedestrian enters the crosswalk perpendicular to the ego vehicle’s path, requiring the ego to yield or stop to avoid a collision.
The adversarial oncoming car maintains its speed or accelerates through the intersection, asserting its right-of-way while the ego vehicle attempts to turn.
The adversarial car suddenly breaks when the ego approaches
The adversarial car suddenly performs a left turn across the ego vehicle's path, forcing the ego to stop.
The adversarial crushed boxes (static debris) act as a road hazard, forcing the ego vehicle to perform an emergency maneuver or brake.
The adversarial pedestrian jaywalks into the road, moving directly into the ego vehicle's path as it approaches.
he adversarial pedestrian unexpectedly crosses the road using standard pedestrian crossing behavior.
The adversarial pedestrian suddenly dashes into the road from behind a parked vehicle and comes to an abrupt stop in the ego vehicle's lane.
The adversarial car travels at a constant speed or accelerates to pass the ego vehicle on the right, creating a conflict when the ego attempts to change lanes.
The adversarial car (slow vehicle) accelerates as the ego attempts to merge back, intentionally blocking the lane change and forcing the ego to interact with a secondary leading vehicle.
The adversarial car jumps a red signal and continues straight through the intersection, creating a collision course with the ego vehicle.
The adversarial vehicle maintains a slow speed while the ego performs an evasive lane change
Handles the pedestrian's reactive crossing behavior. The adversarial pedestrian enters the crosswalk while the ego vehicle is mid-turn, forcing the ego to perform an emergency stop.
The adversarial vehicle ignores traffic signals while the ego maintains safety protocols.
The adversarial car performs a left turn from a perpendicular lane into the ego's path, necessitating emergency braking.
The adversarial car performs a left turn into the same destination lane as the ego vehicle, failing to yield or creating a simultaneous merge conflict.
Pedestrian's crossing response using standard Carla behaviors. The adversarial pedestrian enters the crosswalk on the destination road, requiring the ego vehicle to yield and stop mid-turn.
